text.source_text: इतश्चानन्दमयः पर एवात्मा , नेतरः ; इतर ईश्वरादन्यः संसारी जीव इत्यर्थः । न जीव आनन्दमयशब्देनाभिधीयते । कस्मात् ? अनुपपत्तेः । आनन्दमयं हि प्रकृत्य श्रूयते — ‘ सोऽकामयत । बहु स्यां प्रजायेयेति । स तपोऽतप्यत । स तपस्तप्त्वा । इदꣳ सर्वमसृजत । यदिदं किञ्च ’ (तै. उ. २ । ६ । १) इति । तत्र प्राक्शरीराद्युत्पत्तेरभिध्यानम् , सृज्यमानानां च विकाराणां स्रष्टुरव्यतिरेकः , सर्वविकारसृष्टिश्च न परस्मादात्मनोऽन्यत्रोपपद्यते ॥ १६ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: itaścānandamayaḥ para evātmā , netaraḥ ; itara īśvarādanyaḥ saṃsārī jīva ityarthaḥ | na jīva ānandamayaśabdenābhidhīyate | kasmāt ? anupapatteḥ | ānandamayaṃ hi prakṛtya śrūyate — ‘ so'kāmayata | bahu syāṃ prajāyeyeti | sa tapo'tapyata | sa tapastaptvā | idam̐ sarvamasṛjata | yadidaṃ kiñca ’ (tai. u. 2 | 6 | 1) iti | tatra prākśarīrādyutpatterabhidhyānam , sṛjyamānānāṃ ca vikārāṇāṃ sraṣṭuravyatirekaḥ , sarvavikārasṛṣṭiśca na parasmādātmano'nyatropapadyate || 16 ||

translation.literal_translation: And for this reason too anandamaya is the Supreme Self, not the other; 'the other' means the transmigrating individual self distinct from Ishvara. The jiva is not denoted by anandamaya. Why? Because it is untenable. For after introducing anandamaya, scripture says: 'He desired: may I become many, may I be born; He performed tapas; having done tapas He created all this, whatever there is.' Here intentional resolve prior to body-production, non-difference from the entities to be produced, and creation of all modifications are intelligible only in the Supreme Self, not in another.
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.1.16 excludes jiva-reading: pre-cosmic willing, total creation, and causal non-difference predicates fit only Ishvara/Brahman.
translation.interpretive_note: Creative intentionality is used to distinguish Supreme Self from bound individual self.
